description: Ground-truth for Power-Shift direction. Each edit has an objectively-correct favored party (a lawyer would agree). Used to measure favor-direction accuracy honestly. Parties are always [A, B] as written.

pairs.old: The Provider shall encrypt all customer data.
pairs.new: The Provider may encrypt customer data.
pairs.note: obligation on Provider relaxed -> Provider benefits

pairs.old: The Provider's liability is capped at fees paid.
pairs.note: cap removed -> Client can recover more

pairs.old: The Client shall pay within 30 days.
pairs.new: The Client shall pay within 60 days.
The warranty does not cover defects.

pairs.old: The Client may terminate at any time.
pairs.new: The Client may terminate only for cause.

pairs.old: The Provider must deliver within 7 days.
pairs.new: The Provider must deliver within 30 days.

pairs.old: The Client owns all resulting IP.
pairs.new: The Provider owns all resulting IP.

pairs.old: The Provider indemnifies the Client against claims.
pairs.new: The Client indemnifies the Provider against claims.

pairs.old: The Client may audit the Provider annually.
pairs.new: The Client may not audit the Provider.

pairs.note: longer protection of Client's disclosed info (Client is typically the discloser here)

pairs.new: The Provider grants up to 2 revisions.
